source.quote: Servers that support prompts MUST declare the `prompts` capability during initialization:
When the list of available prompts changes, servers that declared the `listChanged` capability SHOULD send a notification:

requirements.exclusion: The trigger condition — the prompt list actually changing — is server-internal state a recorded trace cannot evidence.

source.quote: The image data MUST be base64-encoded and include a valid MIME type.
The audio data MUST be base64-encoded and include a valid MIME type.
Resources can contain either text or binary (blob) data and MUST include: A valid resource URI; The appropriate MIME type; Either text content or base64-encoded blob data
Implementations MUST carefully validate all prompt inputs and outputs to prevent injection attacks or unauthorized access to resources.

requirements.exclusion: Validation thoroughness is implementation-internal; a trace shows the prompt traffic, not the vetting applied to it.

source.quote: Servers SHOULD return standard JSON-RPC errors for common failure cases: Invalid prompt name: `-32602` (Invalid params); Missing required arguments: `-32602` (Invalid params); Internal errors: `-32603` (Internal error)

requirements.exclusion: Whether a failure case actually occurred (the name was invalid, an argument was missing) is server-side ground truth a trace does not carry.

source.quote: Servers SHOULD validate prompt arguments before processing

requirements.exclusion: Validation thoroughness is implementation-internal (the PROM-006 argument).

source.quote: Clients SHOULD handle pagination for large prompt lists

requirements.exclusion: Client-side pagination handling is internal; a session whose lists fit one page exercises nothing.

source.quote: Both parties SHOULD respect capability negotiation

requirements.exclusion: Mechanized at the protocol level by LIFE-009's negotiated-capabilities-only check; this restatement adds no separately observable conduct.
